images.caption:
Figure 1:We proposeNormGuard, a simple norm-budget regularizer that suppresses
reward-irrelevant norm inflation during RL training of flow-matching models.NormGuardreduces the over-sharpening, color
oversaturation, and unnatural lighting seen in the baseline,
producing more photo-realistic results. Prompts see Appendix10.1.

Figure 2:Motivation: RL post-training inflates the per-step velocity norm and produces visual artifacts (left). Unlike CFG, inference-time renormalization fails for RL because the inflation is co-adapted into the model weights (middle). An
adjoint sensitivity analysis confirms that suppressing norm inflation carries no coherent first-order reward signal, motivating our training-time hinge penalty on excess velocity norm (right).

Figure 3:RL-induced norm inflation.Mean velocity norm across denoising timesteps for AWM, DPO, and NFT on SD3.5-Medium. RL post-training (orange) uniformly inflates the norm above the pretrained reference (blue dashed).NormGuardregularizer (magenta) suppresses the excess without sacrificing reward.

Figure 3:RL-induced norm inflation.Mean velocity norm across denoising timesteps for AWM, DPO, and NFT on SD3.5-Medium. RL post-training (orange) uniformly inflates the norm above the pretrained reference (blue dashed).NormGuardregularizer (magenta) suppresses the excess without sacrificing reward.

Figure 4:Norm-Scaling sensitivityS​(t)S(t)across denoising steps (SD3.5-M + 6.4K samples). The noise-to-signal ratioσ/|μ|\sigma/|\mu|ranges from3×3\timesto100×100\times, indicating noisy signal along the norm inflation direction.
Table 1:Inference-time renormalization does not imporve reward.Δ​R\Delta Rdenotes the change in reward w/ and w/o velocity renormalization.

Table 2:Training configuration.Hyperparameters for all (model, reward, method) combinations.λ\lambdadenotes the velocity-norm regularization strength, andrrdenotes the LoRA rank. Full details are provided in Appendix8.
Figure 6:Reward vs. MLLM quality score.Our regularizer improves quality with minimal reward change.
Figure 7:Qualitative results.We compare samples with and withoutNormGuardregularizer. Prompts see Appendix10.2.
Table 6:KL complementarity(FLUX.2-4B, NFT, PickScore). Our regularizer (λ\lambda) improves RealScore at every KL strength (βKL\beta_{\text{KL}}).
Figure 8:Training curve of PickScore evaluation corresponding to Table6.
Figure 9:PickScore evaluation with different regularizer strengths (λ\lambda).
